rulings.Case Title: BRYAN DIAZ VS. MARIN STREET ENTERPRISES, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 418.10(A)(3), 583.210(A) And 583.250(A)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2024, line 1, DEFENDANT LEGION INDUSTRIES, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS SHIELD CORPS SECURITY Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 418.10(A)(3), 583.210(A) And 583.250(A) Defendant Legion Industries' motion to dismiss is granted. In California, the mandatory time for serving summons and complaint is three years from when the action is filed. (CCP 583.210.) Here, plaintiff reports the action was filed January 15, 2020 and Legion's owner was served at his residence on March 6, 2024 - more than four years later. (Opp. 2:8, 3:5-7.) Plaintiff makes two tolling arguments, both unavailing. First, plaintiff says CCP 583.210 was tolled by Emergency Rule 9. (Opp. 3:8-11.) But that rule's plain language applies it solely to "statutes of limitations and repose," not to service of summons and complaint. Second, plaintiff says timely service was impossible. (Opp. 4:4.) But the fact that plaintiff served Legion's owner at his residence in March 2024 demonstrates possibility. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MISSION BAY BLOCK 7 HOUSING PARTNERS, L.P. A VS. NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment And Or Alternatively For Summary Adjudication On Egan Plumbing Incs Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2024, line 8, 4 - CROSS DEFENDANT TOMMY SIU DBA TOMMY SIU & ASSOCIATES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment And Or Alternatively For Summary Adjudication On Egan Plumbing Incs Cross Complaint Tommy Siu d/b/a Tommy Siu & Associates' (TSA) motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication of Egan Plumbing Inc.'s cross complaint is denied. This motion mirrors TSA's motion for summary judgement as to general contractor Nibbi Bros. Associates, Inc's cross complaint. The parties do not dispute that the same indemnification clause controls, as subcontractor Egan incorporated all provisions of the prime contract into its subcontract. Moreover, the briefs rehash the same legal arguments. Accordingly, for the same reasons provided in the companion order, the court denies this motion as well. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2024, line 8, 2 - CROSS DEFENDANT TOMMY SIU DBA TOMMY SIU & ASSOCIATES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Its Second And Third Causes Of Action Regarding Duty To Defend Obligations As Against Cross Defendant Egan Plumbing, Inc. Tommy Siu d/b/a Tommy Siu & Associates' (TSA) motion for summary adjudication as to the issue of duty within the second and third causes of action of its cross complaint against Egan Plumbing Inc. is denied. This motion mirrors TSA's motion for summary adjudication on the issue of duty regarding its claims against general contractor Nibbi Bros. Associates, Inc. The parties do not dispute that the same indemnification clause controls, as subcontractor Egan incorporated all provisions of the prime contract into its subcontract. Moreover, the briefs rehash the same legal arguments. Accordingly, for the same reasons provided in the companion order, the court denies this motion as well. rulings.Case Title: SAMEER BHADOURIA ET AL VS. MSASA PROPERITES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2024, line 5, DEFENDANT MSASA PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Continued to September 23, 2024. Plaintiffs shall provide a courtesy copy of their appendix of evidence that is bound in compliance with CRC 3.1110(e). Loose papers are not workable and the court has plaintiffs' other papers. Defendant shall provide a courtesy copy of its moving papers in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. The court only has a copy of defendant's notice of errata re the points and authorities. The parties are to attach a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. = (302/RBU)
